labs.frequency: within 2-4 weeks after initiation or dose increase
labs.initialMonitoring: within 2-4 weeks of initiation or increase in dose
labs.maintenanceFrequency: within 2-4 weeks after each dose change, then annually
labs.actionRequired: Continue if <30% increase; reduce dose or stop if >30% increase
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for acute kidney injury and assess tolerability of ACEi therapy

labs.frequency: within 2-4 weeks after initiation or dose increase
labs.initialMonitoring: within 2-4 weeks of initiation or increase in dose
labs.maintenanceFrequency: within 2-4 weeks after each dose change, then annually
labs.actionRequired: Manage hyperkalemia with potassium reduction measures before dose reduction
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for hyperkalemia, which occurs in up to 10% of outpatients and up to 38% of hospitalized patients

labs.frequency: within 2-4 weeks after initiation or dose increase
labs.initialMonitoring: within 2-4 weeks of initiation or increase in dose
labs.actionRequired: Discontinue other BP medications before reducing ACEi dose if symptomatic hypotension occurs

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: Close monitoring of serum potassium required when eGFR < 30 ml/min per 1.73 m2. Contraindicated in pregnancy - discontinue in women considering pregnancy or who become pregnant.
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: Monitor for changes in blood pressure, serum creatinine, and serum potassium within 2–4 weeks of initiation or increase in the dose of an ACEi or ARB. Continue ACEi or ARB therapy unless serum creatinine rises by more than 30% within 4 weeks following initiation of treatment or an increase in dose.
